programme.title: The Caine Mutiny
programme.desc: Set aboard fictional minesweeper, the Caine, during World War II, rich kid Willis Seward Keith (Robert Francis) reports for duty fresh out of officer training school. He is shocked to discover that the ship's captain, Lt. Commander DeVriess (Tom Tully), has let discipline on the ship slide and allows his men to exhibit a casual attitude to their duties. What Keith fails to notice, however, is that the crew of the Caine are in fact excellent at their jobs. But life aboard changes drastically when DeVriess is replaced by veteran no-nonsense captain, Queeg (Humphrey Bogart).

programme.title: Heartbreak Ridge
programme.desc: Hard-nosed, hard-living Marine Gunnery Sergeant Tom Highway clashes with his superiors and his ex-wife as he takes command of a spoiled recon platoon with a bad attitude.

programme.title: But I'm a Cheerleader
programme.desc: Parents send their teenager (Natasha Lyonne) to a rehabilitation camp in the desert because they think she is a lesbian.

programme.title: Mia And The White Lion
programme.desc: Ten-year-old Mia develops a special bond with a beautiful white lion named Charlie. When Mia uncovers a secret that puts Charlie in danger, she sets out on an incredible journey across the plains of Africa to find a sanctuary for her beloved friend.

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: Some of the biggest film stars in history rose to fame during the 'golden age' of Hollywood, in the early and mid-20th century. We profile some of these stars who became household names.

programme.title: One of Our Aircraft Is Missing
programme.desc: On its way back from a raid on the city of Stuttgart, Germany, a British bomber is shot down over Nazi-held Holland. Parachuting into Dutch farmlands under cover of darkness, the six-member crew connects with members of the local resistance, who shelter the Brits from their Nazi inquisitors as they make their way towards freedom.

programme.title: Le Samourai
programme.desc: After carrying out a flawlessly planned hit, Jef Costello, a contract killer with samurai instincts, finds himself caught between a persistent police investigator and a ruthless employer, and not even his armor of fedora and trench coat can protect him.

programme.title: The Gentlemen
programme.desc: The Gentlemen follows American expat Mickey Pearson, whose London marijuana empire sparks schemes, bribery, and betrayal.

programme.title: The Salamander
programme.desc: After her father's death, 40-year-old Catherine escapes to Brazil, where a fling with a young man turns into a passionate affair - forcing her to confront desire, freedom, and tough choices.

programme.title: A Midnight Clear
programme.desc: December 1944, Ardennes Forest: Six US soldiers occupy a house for observation, but the expected German advance is met with surprisingly friendly troops.

programme.title: A Girl Returned
programme.desc: In 1975, a 13-year-old girl is abruptly returned to her birth family, losing her old life of comfort. In poverty, she forms a bond with her sister Adriana and faces harsh new realities.

programme.title: The Movie Show
programme.desc: Classic film review series hosted by legends Margaret Pomeranz and David Stratton. In this episode they review Series7 and interview director Daniel Mainhan, Head Over Heels, Serenades and interview director Mojgan Khaden and actor Aden Young.

programme.title: Le Samourai
programme.desc: After carrying out a flawlessly planned hit, Jef Costello, a contract killer with samurai instincts, finds himself caught between a persistent police investigator and a ruthless employer, and not even his armor of fedora and trench coat can protect him.

programme.title: Discovering Film
programme.desc: Some of the biggest film stars in history rose to fame during the 'golden age' of Hollywood, in the early and mid-20th century. We profile some of these stars who became household names.

programme.title: A United Kingdom
programme.desc: The story of Oxford-educated King Seretse Khama of Botswana and how his loving but controversial marriage to white Englishwoman Ruth Williams in 1948 plunged his kingdom into political and diplomatic turmoil.

programme.title: Wonderfully Beautiful
programme.desc: Following five very different women and their daunting task of finding out what beauty is all about. As mothers, daughters and girlfriends, the women search for new body awareness.

programme.title: 20,000 Species of Bees
programme.desc: An eight-year-old child struggles with the fact that people keep addressing her in confusing ways. During a summer in the Basque Country among the beehives, she explores her identity alongside the women of her family, who at the same time reflect on their own lives and desires.

programme.title: The Red Turtle
programme.desc: Gorgeously animated, this wordless story about a stranded castaway pays tribute to what's most important in life: companionship, love, family, and the stewardship of nature.

programme.title: Viceroy's House
programme.desc: In 1947, Lord Mountbatten assumes the post of last viceroy, charged with handing India back to its people, living upstairs at the house which was the home of British rulers, while 500 Hindu, Muslim and Sikh servants lived downstairs.

programme.title: Loophole
programme.desc: Unlikely partners Mike Daniels, an expert safe cracker and failed architect Stephen Booker prepare to extract a fortune in cash and jewels from the vault of one of London's largest holdings banks. After months of painstaking planning it seems their plan cannot fail. Disaster however can come in the strangest of places and an unexpected thunderstorm alters both of their plans.

programme.title: Snatch
programme.desc: Unscrupulous boxing promoters, violent bookmakers, a Russian gangster, incompetent amateur robbers and jewellers fight amongst themselves to track down a priceless stolen diamond. Directed by Guy Ritchie and features and all-star cast, including Brad Pitt, Jason Statham, Benicio Del Toro and Stephen Graham.

programme.title: The Invisible Wounds
programme.desc: Camille has disappeared. Was it murder, suicide, or did she run away? Two investigations are launched: one by the police and the other one by Camille's best friend Marion. The testimonies of those who knew Camille are conflicting, painting a contrasting portrait of this married woman and mother of two young girls.

programme.title: The Gentlemen
programme.desc: The Gentlemen follows American expat Mickey Pearson, whose London marijuana empire sparks schemes, bribery, and betrayal.

programme.title: The Salamander
programme.desc: After her father's death, 40-year-old Catherine escapes to Brazil, where a fling with a young man turns into a passionate affair - forcing her to confront desire, freedom, and tough choices.

programme.title: But I'm a Cheerleader
programme.desc: Parents send their teenager (Natasha Lyonne) to a rehabilitation camp in the desert because they think she is a lesbian.

programme.title: The Movie Show
programme.desc: Margaret Pomeranz talks to actor Sean Scully about his career. David Stratton interviews Soviet actress Svetlana Kopylova about My Name is Harlequin and her role in the film.

programme.title: Viceroy's House
programme.desc: In 1947, Lord Mountbatten assumes the post of last viceroy, charged with handing India back to its people, living upstairs at the house which was the home of British rulers, while 500 Hindu, Muslim and Sikh servants lived downstairs.

programme.title: Loophole
programme.desc: Unlikely partners Mike Daniels, an expert safe cracker and failed architect Stephen Booker prepare to extract a fortune in cash and jewels from the vault of one of London's largest holdings banks. After months of painstaking planning it seems their plan cannot fail. Disaster however can come in the strangest of places and an unexpected thunderstorm alters both of their plans.

programme.title: Ghoomer
programme.desc: Batting prodigy Anina loses her hand on the eve of her international cricketing debut. A failed cricketer enters her life and with innovative training, she becomes a bowler.

programme.title: Manon Des Sources
programme.desc: The stunning sequel to Jean de Florette. Manon, a beautiful young shepherdess living in the idyllic Provencal countryside, discovers the truth surrounding her father's death and decides to take revenge upon the men responsible.
